31 January 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Statement on new phase in Venezuela Spain MFA 31 Jan 2026

Defense Policy / Programs

  • NATO Secretary General visits Allied Air Command in Ramstein NATO Allied Air Command 31 Jan 2026 -- NATO's Secretary General Mark Rutte visited Allied Air Command on January 29th to observe the Command's round-the-clock role in safeguarding every inch of NATO and its contributions to strengthening deterrence, especially along NATO's eastern flank.

  • Government improves procedures for reserved personnel status at critical defense industry enterprises Ukraine MOD 31 Jan 2026
  • Ukraine and NATO agree on cooperation priorities: air defense, F-16s, HIMARS, and technological edge Ukraine MOD 31 Jan 2026

  • Official Chinese media releases rare footage of J-20A stealth fighter jets in test flights Global Times 31 Jan 2026 -- Official Chinese media on Saturday provided details on rarely seen systematic test flights featuring newly manufactured J-20A stealth fighter jets, with an expert saying that the Chinese manufacturer has reached a new level in aircraft production.

  • Iran, China, and Russia to hold joint naval exercise Press TV 31 Jan 2026 -- Iran, Russia, and China are set to conduct a joint naval exercise in the northern Indian Ocean in late February, amid heightened tensions between Tehran and Washington.

  • US approves arms sales to Israel worth $6.67 billion and to Saudi Arabia worth $9 billion Press TV 31 Jan 2026 -- The US State Department has approved massive new series of military sales to Israel totaling more than $6.5 billion in three separate contracts and to Saudi Arabia worth $9 billion, the Pentagon says.
  • U.S. approves massive new arms sales to Israel, Saudi Arabia People's Daily 31 Jan 2026 -- The White House has approved massive new arms sales totaling 15.67 billion U.S. dollars to Israel and Saudi Arabia, two major U.S. allies in the Middle East, amid tensions over Iran's unrest, the U.S. State Department said in a statement on Friday.

  • Washington approves over $6.5 billion military sales to Tel Aviv IRNA 31 Jan 2026 -- The US Department of Defense has announced the State Department's approval of a $6.67 billion weapons package sale to Israel, encompassing three separate contracts.

  • Nations Support Panamanian Rule of Law US Dept. of State 31 Jan 2026 -- "The undersigned nations take note of Panama's Supreme Court decision to declare CK Hutchison's concession of the Balboa and Cristobal ports unconstitutional."
  • Secretary Rubio's Call with Brazilian Foreign Minister Vieira US Dept. of State 31 Jan 2026

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • UN Security Council Meeting (Arria Formula) on Upholding the Sanctity of Treaties for the Maintenance of International Peace and Security Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 31 Jan 2026 -- Pakistan organized an "Arria-formula"meeting of the UN Security Council on the topic "Upholding the Sanctity of Treaties for the Maintenance of International Peace and Security". In addition to Security Council members, the meeting was attended by Member States from all regions.

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Israeli Strikes on Gaza Kill Over 30 People Since Early Saturday - Reports Sputnik 31 Jan 2026 -- At least 31 people have been killed in Israeli airstrikes on the Gaza Strip since early Saturday, media reported on Saturday, citing medical sources.
  • Death toll from Israeli attacks across Gaza rises to 29 since dawn Press TV 31 Jan 2026 -- Israeli forces have killed at least 29 Palestinians across the Gaza Strip amid reports of attacks in Rafah, in yet another violation of the ceasefire agreement.

  • One killed as fresh Israeli airstrike hits southern Lebanon amid ceasefire violations Press TV 31 Jan 2026 -- In another near-daily violation of a ceasefire agreement with Lebanon which went into effect in November 2024, the Israeli military has conducted an airstrike in the southern part of the Arab country, killing at least one person.

  • Israeli claims of ceasefire violations meant to 'justify massacres' in Gaza: Hamas Press TV 31 Jan 2026 -- Hamas has roundly dismissed Israeli claims that recent bombings in Gaza are a "response" to the Palestinian resistance movement's violations of the ceasefire agreement, emphasizing that such allegations "are nothing but a blatant and pathetic attempt to justify the horrific massacres against civilians" in the coastal sliver.

  • Trump's Board of Peace serves personal interests, undermines UN: Special rapporteur IRNA 31 Jan 2026 -- UN Special Rapporteur on human rights in the occupied Palestinian Territories Francesca Albanese has criticized the so-called Board of Peace, stating it serves US President Donald Trump's personal interests and is designed as a parallel body to the United Nations.

  • US envoys blocked warning of Gaza turning into 'apocalyptic wasteland': Report Press TV 31 Jan 2026 -- US envoys in occupied Palestine prevented a series of 2024 memorandums by the US Agency for International Development (USAID) from reaching the Joe Biden administration, silencing warnings about Gaza's rapid collapse, according to a report.

  • No: 23, 31 January 2026, Regarding Israel's Attacks on the Gaza Strip Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 31 Jan 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Chinese military conducts combat readiness patrols around Huangyan Dao PRC MND 31 Jan 2026 -- The Chinese military on Saturday conducted combat readiness patrols in the territorial waters and airspace of China's Huangyan Dao and the surrounding maritime and aerial areas.
  • H-6K bomber joins PLA sea-air combat readiness patrol in waters and airspace near China's Huangyan Dao Global Times 31 Jan 2026 -- The Southern Theater Command of the Chinese People's Liberation Army (PLA) organized naval and air forces to conduct a combat readiness patrol on Saturday in the territorial waters and airspace of Huangyan Dao and surrounding areas, according to a statement published by the official WeChat account of the PLA Southern Theater Command on Saturday.

  • On 31 January 2026, terrorists of Indian sponsored Fitna al Hindustan attempted to disturb peace of Balochistan by conducting multiple terrorist activities around Quetta, Mastung, Nushki, Dalbandin, Kharan, Panjgur, Tump, Gwadar and Pasni. ISPR Pakistan 31 Jan 2026
  • No: 22, 31 January 2026, Regarding the Terrorist Attacks in Pakistan Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 31 Jan 2026

  • Myanmar: Statement by the High Representative on behalf of the European Union on the fifth anniversary of the coup EC/CEU 31 Jan 2026
  • Statement by Minister Anand five years following military coup in Myanmar Canada 31 Jan 2026

  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary General - on Ethiopia UN Secretary-General 31 Jan 2026
  • Spokesperson statement: worrying developments in Northern Ethiopia European External Action Service (EEAS) 31 Jan 2026 -- We have been following with great concern recent developments in Northern Ethiopia that have included military confrontations and clashes.

  • Press Release by the Ministry of Foreign Affairs regarding the 2815/2026 UN Security Council Resolution on the renewal of the mandate of the UN Peacekeeping Force in Cyprus Republic of Cyprus 31 Jan 2026 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs welcomes the adoption of the 2815/2026 United Nations (UN) Security Council Resolution on the renewal of the mandate of the United Nation Peacekeeping Force in Cyprus (UNFICYP) for another 12 months, until January 31 2027.

  • The 2026 Tartu Peace Treaty seminar focused on the UN's role in shaping international law Estonia MFA 31 Jan 2026 -- Yesterday, 30 January, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs hosted the 19th annual conference dedicated to the Tartu Peace Treaty. This year's seminar focused on the United Nations (UN), which marked its 80th anniversary last year, and on the organisation's impact on the development of international law.

  • No: 21, 31 January 2026, Regarding UN Security Council Resolution No. 2815 (2026) on the Extension of the Mandate of the UN Peacekeeping Force in the Island of Cyprus Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 31 Jan 2026

News Reports

  • DNI Gabbard at National Sheriffs' Association Winter Conference: Delivering Intelligence To America's Front Line Director of National Intelligence 31 Jan 2026 -- Today, Director of National Intelligence (DNI) Tulsi Gabbard addressed the National Sheriffs' Association Winter Conference, reaffirming the Intelligence Community's commitment to arm law enforcement with intelligence as they continue to serve as America's front line against foreign cartels and gangs, radical extremists, and national security threats.

  • Xi, Algerian president exchange congratulations over successful launch of Algerian satellite PRC MND 31 Jan 2026 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping on Saturday exchanged congratulations with Algerian President Abdelmadjid Tebboune over the successful launch of an Algerian remote sensing satellite from Jiuquan in northwest China.

  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 31 Jan 2026

  • US Refuses to Share Plans Regarding Iran With Allies in Persian Gulf - Reports Sputnik 31 Jan 2026 -- The US administration has not shared its goals and plans regarding Iran with its allies in the Persian Gulf, including during consultations with representatives of Saudi Arabia in Washington "aimed at gaining clarity," Fox News reported, citing a high-ranking official from a Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) state.
  • Diplomacy Iran's priority, but forced negotiations unacceptable: President Press TV 31 Jan 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian has reiterated that resolving disputes through diplomacy remains Iran's priority, but notes that the Islamic Republic cannot be compelled into negotiations through threats or coercion and that war would benefit neither side either.
  • Araghchi: US must abandon threats; Iran ready to respond to aggression Press TV 31 Jan 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the United States has to abandon threats and pressures against Iran for genuine and fruitful negotiations to open, but stressed that the Islamic Republic is fully prepared to deliver a severe and regret-inducing response to any act of aggression.
  • Head of Iran's Powerful National Security Council Says Conditions For Talks Forming Despite War Hype Sputnik 31 Jan 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said Friday that Iran is ready for both dialogue and war, and expressed hope that the US would "act in a sense of rationality."
  • Iran Has Sufficient Strength for Self-Defense, Ready for Any Scenario - Araghchi Sputnik 31 Jan 2026 -- Iran possesses sufficient strength for self-defense and is ready for any scenario amid threats from the United States, Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on Saturday.
  • Army chief says Iran's defense capabilities are robust. IRNA 31 Jan 2026 -- The chief of the Army of the Islamic Republic of Iran says the country's defensive power has become indigenous due to sanctions and is indestructible.
  • 'Our hands are on the trigger': Iranian army chief warns Israel, US against mischief Press TV 31 Jan 2026 -- Iran's top army commander says the country's armed forces are fully prepared for any military confrontation, warning that any misstep by Israel or the US would endanger their own security and destabilize the region.
  • Assembly of Experts condemns EU's move in blacklisting IRGC IRNA 31 Jan 2026 -- The Assembly of Experts has condemned the European Union's decision to designate the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization.
  • EU's IRGC terror label shows force's strategic importance: Expediency Council IRNA 31 Jan 2026 -- Head of Iran's Expediency Council says the European Union's "hostile action" of designating the IRGC a terrorist organization demonstrates the force's strategic importance in protecting the country.
  • Guardian Council: IRGC is symbol of national power in Iran IRNA 31 Jan 2026 -- The Guardian Council has condemned the European Union's decision to designate the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization, stating that IRGC is a symbol of national power in the country.
  • FM: IRGC upholds peace, stability as foreign meddling fuels escalation Press TV 31 Jan 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has played a consistent role in maintaining peace and stability in the Persian Gulf region and the Strait of Hormuz within it, while warning that the presence and actions of extra-regional forces have historically driven escalation rather than de-escalation.
  • IRGC is on frontline against US, Israeli terrorism: Intelligence Ministry Press TV 31 Jan 2026 -- Iran's Ministry of Intelligence says the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) remains on the frontline of confronting the "state terrorism" of the United States and Israel, amid growing pressure and hostile actions against the force.
  • EU's IRGC listing shows elite force effective in foiling Israeli plots: Yemen Press TV 31 Jan 2026 -- The deputy foreign minister of Yemen's Government of Change and Construction has strongly condemned the European Union Council's decision to designate Iran's Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization, saying the move shows the elite force's effectiveness in thwarting Israeli plots in West Asia.
  • IRGC denies report of its navy chief's assassination IRNA 31 Jan 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has dismissed as false a social media report claiming the assassination of its naval commander, Brigadier General Alireza Tangsiri.
  • Iran's top security official, Russian president discuss economic cooperation, regional issues in Moscow Press TV 31 Jan 2026 -- Iran's top security official and Russian President Vladimir Putin have held senior-level talks during an official visit by the former to Moscow.
  • Trump administration targets senior Iranian officials with new sanctions People's Daily 31 Jan 2026 -- The Trump administration on Friday announced new sanctions against a number of Iranian officials, including Iranian Interior Minister Eskandar Momeni, amid the unrest across the Middle Eastern country.
  • U.S.-Iran tensions raise regional concerns as Tehran signals willingness for talks People's Daily 31 Jan 2026 -- Rising tensions between Iran and the United States have alarmed regional leaders and sparked urgent calls for renewed diplomacy. Tehran on Friday signaled a conditional willingness to return to the negotiating table to ease the escalating confrontation.

  • Iraq PM candidate Maliki warns against foreign 'interference' in internal affairs IRNA 31 Jan 2026 -- Iraq's main candidate for the premiership, Nouri al-Maliki, has once again slammed foreign interference and pressure in the country's internal affairs, saying that choosing leaders and government is "a national matter" that must be respected.

  • PM press statement alongside Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi: 31 January 2026 Gov.UK 31 Jan 2026
  • Starmer says 'it would be foolhardy' not to engage with China, refuting Trump's claim of UK's trade cooperation with China Global Times 31 Jan 2026 -- British Prime Minister Keir Starmer said on Friday it would be foolish for Britain not to engage with China, rejecting the assertion from US President Donald Trump that it was dangerous to get into business with Beijing, Reuters reported. Starmer also added in a causal tone that Trump himself was also set to visit China in April.

  • Czech Government Looks For Its Footing On Europe's New US-China Tightrope RFE/RL 31 Jan 2026 -- New Czech Prime Minister Andrej Babis's government has signaled interest in repairing ties with Beijing after years of strained relations.

  • Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi receives a delegation of Arab Foreign Ministers (January 31, 2026) India MEA 31 Jan 2026 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi received a delegation of Foreign Ministers of Arab countries, Secretary General of the League of Arab States and Heads of Arab delegations, who are in India for the second India-Arab Foreign Ministers' Meeting.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova's answer to media question regarding US administration's measures against Cuba Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 31 Jan 2026
  • Russia Condemns Illegitimate US Sanctions Against Cuba and Pressure on Leadership Sputnik 31 Jan 2026 -- The US emergency regime against Cuba marks radical return to strategy of pressure on country, Russian Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Maria Zakharova said.
